cues.label: Structure
cues.headline: Decision tree of splits
cues.narration: Each node represents a split point. At position i, try every end position j. If s[i..j] is a palindrome, add to path and recurse.

cues.label: Base case
cues.headline: Start equals length
cues.narration: When start equals the string length, the current path is a complete valid partition. Copy it into the result list.

cues.label: Un-choose
cues.headline: Pop after recursion
cues.narration: After the recursive call returns, pop the last substring from the path. This is the backtracking step that resets state for the next branch.

cues.label: Palindrome check
cues.headline: Inline vs precomputed
cues.narration: Inline two-pointer check is O(n) per call. Precomputed DP table is O(1) per call after O(n^2) setup. For n up to 16, both work.

cues.label: DP table
cues.headline: Fill diagonals outward
cues.narration: Base: every single character is a palindrome. Then pairs: dp[i][i+1] is true if s[i] equals s[i+1]. Then length 3 and up: dp[i][j] = (s[i] == s[j]) and dp[i+1][j-1].

cues.label: Optimization
cues.headline: Bottleneck is output size
cues.narration: After precomputation, the backtracking cost is proportional to the number of valid partitions times n for path copying. For n = 16, this is at most 32K partitions.

cues.label: Template
cues.headline: Backtracking = choose-explore-unchoose
cues.narration: This pattern applies to every enumeration problem: combinations, permutations, subsets, N-queens, Sudoku. The only thing that changes is the validity check.

cues.label: Recognition
cues.headline: 'All possible' is the signal
cues.narration: When a problem says 'return all possible,' 'find every valid,' or 'list all,' and the constraint is small, you are looking at backtracking.
